# Swift Package Manager | Documentation

Framework# Swift Package Manager

Organize, manage, and edit Swift packages.## [Overview](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/#Overview)

The Swift Package Manager lets you share your code as a package, depend on and use other shared packages, as well as build, test, document, and run your code.

Note

Swift Package Manager is previewing the Swift Build build system. Learn more [here](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftbuildpreview).

## [Topics](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/#topics)

### [Essentials](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/#Essentials)

[Getting Started](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/gettingstarted)Learn to create and use Swift packages.[Introducing Packages](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/introducingpackages)Learn to create and use a Swift package.[Package Security](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/packagesecurity)Learn about the security features that the package manager implements.### [Guides](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/#Guides)

[[6.3] Preview the Swift Build System Integration](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftbuildpreview)Understand, use, and preview the next-generation build system for Package Manager.[Creating a Swift package](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/creatingswiftpackage)Bundle executable or shareable code into a standalone Swift package.[Setting the Swift tools version](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/settingswifttoolsversion)Define the minimum version of the swift compiler required for your package.[Adding dependencies to a Swift package](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/addingdependencies)Use other Swift packages, system libraries, or binary dependencies in your package.[Resolving and updating dependencies](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/resolvingpackageversions)Coordinate and constrain dependencies for your package.[Creating C language targets](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/creatingclanguagetargets)Include C language code as a target in your Swift package.[Using build configurations](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/usingbuildconfigurations)Control the build configuration for your app or package.[Packaging based on the version of Swift](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftversionspecificpackaging)Provide a package manifest for a specific version of Swift.[Bundling resources with a Swift package](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/bundlingresources)Add resource files to your Swift package and access them in your code.[Releasing and publishing a Swift package](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/releasingpublishingapackage)Share a specific version of your package.[Generating Software Bill of Materials (SBOM)](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/generatingsboms)Create an inventory of components and dependencies using SBOM documents.[Continuous Integration Workflows](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/continuousintegration)Build Swift packages with an existing continuous integration setup and prepare apps that consume package dependencies within an existing CI pipeline.[Plugins](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/plugins)Extend package manager functionality with build or command plugins.[Module Aliasing](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/modulealiasing)Create aliased names for modules to avoid collisions between targets in your package or its dependencies.[Using a package registry](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/usingswiftpackageregistry)Configure and use a package registry for Swift Package Manager.[Package Collections](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/packagecollections)Learn to create, publish and use Swift package collections.[Using shell completion scripts](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/usingshellcompletion)Customize your shell to automatically complete swift package commands.[Swift Package Manager as a library](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftpmasalibrary)Include Swift Package Manager as a dependency in your Swift package.### [Swift Commands](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/#Swift-Commands)

[swift build](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftbuild)Build sources into binary products.[swift test](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swifttest)Build and run tests.[swift package](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftpackagecommands)Subcommands to update and inspect your Swift package.[swift sdk](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftsdkcommands)Perform operations on Swift SDKs.[swift package-registry](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftpackageregistrycommands)Interact with package registry and manage related configuration.[swift package-collection](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftpackagecollectioncommands)Interact with package collections.[swift run](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/swiftrun)Build and run an executable product.### [Design](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/#Design)

[Swift Package Registry Service Specification](/swiftpm/documentation/packagemanagerdocs/registryserverspecification)Learn about the specification for SwiftPM’s registry service.
